title: Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back Original Soundtrack
content:
{{Top|rwm|ref|title=''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'' Original Soundtrack}}
{{Soundtrack
|image=[[File:Empire_LP_80.jpg]]
|title=''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'' Original Soundtrack
|composer=[[John Williams]] (composer/conductor)
|conductor=
|performer=[[London Symphony Orchestra]]
|producer=
|director=

|publisher=[[Wikipedia:RSO Records|RSO Records]]
|release date=[[1980]]<ref name="Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back Original Soundtrack">[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back Original Soundtrack|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'' Original Soundtrack]]</ref>
|media type=[[Wikipedia:Phonograph record|Record]]

'''''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'' Original Soundtrack''' was composed by [[John Williams]], was recorded in 18 sessions at the [[Wikipedia:Anvil Studios|Anvil Studios]] and the [[Wikipedia:Abbey Road Studios|Abbey Road Studios]] over three days in December 1979 and a further six days in January 1980 by the [[London Symphony Orchestra]]. Between [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] and ''The Empire Strikes Back'', Williams had also used the London Symphony Orchestra for the film's score of [[Wikipedia:The Fury (film)|''The Fury'']], [[Wikipedia:Superman music|''Superman'']] and [[Wikipedia:Dracula (1979 film)|''Dracula'']]. The score won another [[Wikipedia:Academy Awards|Oscar]] nomination for Williams. Again, the score was orchestrated by [[Herbert W. Spencer]] and [[Angela Morley]], recorded by engineer [[Eric Tomlinson]] and edited by [[Ken Wannberg]] with supervision by [[Lionel Newman]].

The soundtrack was first released as a 75-minute double LP five days before the premiere of the film but the first compact disc release ran only half the length of the 2 LP set.

==Background==

Just like with [[Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope Original Soundtrack|the first film's OST]], the album for Empire was assembled by music editor [[Ken Wannberg]] under the directions of John Williams. For this OST, Williams personally selected 75 minutes of music, which were edited down and rearranged into a program designed for a "pleasant listening experience". Once again, the cues on this album do not use the film mixes made by [[Eric Tomlinson]], but rather were remixed from the 24-track masters by [[John Neal]]. This would be the last Star Wars album where this was done, as Return of the Jedi's OST would utilize the film mixes.<ref name="Chris Malone document">{{WebCite|url=https://www.malonedigital.com/starwars.pdf|text=Recording the Star Wars Saga|author=Malone, Chris|date=2012-03-18|format=PDF|work=[https://www.malonedigital.com Malone Digital]|archivedate=20220619203126}}</ref> Interestingly, the European version of the Empire OST had a different program than the US one. As it was only a single LP, it featured only 40 minutes of score instead of 75, and it was also reordered for unknown reasons.<ref name="discogs uk lp">{{WebCite|url=https://www.discogs.com/release/2723292-John-Williams-4London-Symphony-Orchestra-Star-Wars-The-Empire-Strikes-Back-The-Original-Soundtrack-F|text=John Williams (4), The London Symphony Orchestra – Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (The Original Soundtrack From The Motion Picture)|archivedate=20220703144741}}</ref> The tracks themselves are all identical in content to the US program, as they came from the same masters.<ref name="Chris Malone document" />

The European OST program was later released on CD everywhere by [[Wikipedia:Polydor|''Polydor'']] in 1985. Unfortunately, since they used the European program, the Polydor CDs only feature 40 minutes worth of music. This release used the exact same masters as the original LP, so it contains the same edits and the same John Neal mixes.<ref name="Chris Malone document" /> While this release was a nice quality upgrade over the original LP, it was not a direct replacement of it due to the missing music.<ref name="discogs polydor cd">{{WebCite|url=https://www.discogs.com/release/10536519-John-Williams-4-The-London-Symphony-Orchestra-The-Empire-Strikes-Back|text=John Williams (4) / The London Symphony Orchestra – The Empire Strikes Back|archivedate=20220703145403}}</ref>

In 2004, [[Wikipedia:Sony Classical Records|Sony Classical]] acquired the rights to the classic trilogy scores since it already had the rights to release the second trilogy soundtracks (''[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (soundtrack)|The Phantom Menace]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones (soundtrack)|Attack of the Clones]]''). In 2004, Sony Classical re-pressed [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back Special Edition: Original Motion Picture Soundtrack|the 1997 RCA Victor release of the Special Edition Star Wars trilogy, including ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. The set was released in a less-than-spectacular package with the new art work mirroring the film's [[Star Wars Trilogy (DVD)|first DVD release]]. Despite the Sony digital remastering, which minimally improved the sound heard only on high-end stereos, this 2004 release is essentially the 1997 RCA Victor release.<ref name="SWArchive-Sony Classical Presents Star Wars Trilogy Soundtracks on CD">{{SWArchive|url=collecting/news/misc/news20040820.html|text=Sony Classical Presents ''Star Wars '' Trilogy Soundtracks on CD}}</ref>

The original American OST masters were finally made available in a format other than vinyl in 2016 by Sony Classical, who rereleased them as part of the "Ultimate Digital Collection" which came out soon before they lost the rights. This release was sourced from a new high quality scan of the OST master, so it still featured the John Neal mixes and all the original edits. However, the Ultimate Digital Collection was available from sites like [[Wikipedia:HDTracks|HDTracks]] in lossless up to 192 kHz/24-bit, where the Polydor CD would've been standard CD quality which is lossless 44.1 kHz/16-bit. Plus, since it featured the American program and not the European one, it has 35 minutes of additional music that were finally available in digital for the first time (at least in their John Neal mixes).<ref name="jwfan post about Ultimate Digital Collection">{{WebCite|url=https://www.jwfan.com/?p=8596|text=JWFan Reviews 'Star Wars: The Ultimate Soundtrack Collection'|author=Ojala, Mikko|date=2016-01-18|archivedate=20210125010049}}</ref><ref name="SW-Sony Classical to Release Ultimate Editions of Original Star Wars Soundtracks">{{SW|url=news/sony-classical-to-release-ultimate-editions-of-original-star-wars-soundtracks|text=Sony Classical to Release Ultimate Editions of Original ''Star Wars'' Soundtracks|nolive=1}}</ref><ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.masterworksmusic.com/star-wars-ultimate-vinyl-cd-digital-box-set-force-awakens-soundtrack-today|text=STAR WARS: THE ULTIMATE VINYL, CD AND DIGITAL BOX SET | THE FORCE AWAKENS SOUNDTRACK OUT TODAY|archivedate=20151231020135}}</ref><ref>{{WebCite|access-date=August 12, 2023|url=https://www.sony.com/content/sony/en/en_us/SCA/company-news/press-releases/sony-music-entertainment/2015/star-wars-the-ultimate-editions-of-the-original-film-soundtracks.html|text=Star Wars™ - The Ultimate Editions Of The Original Film Soundtracks|date=2015-09-28|work=Sony Music Masterworks|archivedate=20230814185222}}</ref> [[Star Wars: The Ultimate Digital Collection|The Ultimate Digital Collection]] is no longer available for sale, and hasn't been ever since [[Wikipedia:Walt Disney Records|Walt Disney Records]] took over the rights.

On May 4, 2018, Walt Disney Records released a newly-remastered edition of the original 1980 album program on CD, digital download, and streaming services. supervised by [[Wikipedia: Shawn Murphy (sound engineer)|Shawn Murphy]] and [[Skywalker Sound]], this version was transferred to a new hi-resolution (24/192).<ref name="SW-Remasters of the First 6 Star Wars Soundtracks Coming May 4">{{SW|url=news/remasters-of-the-first-6-star-wars-soundtracks-coming-may-4|text=Remasters of the First 6 ''Star Wars'' Soundtracks Coming May 4}}</ref> This remaster was newly assembled from the highest-quality tapes available, rather than sourced from the existing 1980 album masters.<ref name="disney marketing video on remasters">{{OfficialYouTube|channel=@DisneyMusicVEVO|name=Disney Music VEVO|video=WSOzvCtaHmg|text=John Williams - Star Wars: Remastering The Score}}</ref> This release marks the first release on CD of the complete 1980 soundtrack album.<ref name=":1">{{WebCite|publication-place=Burbank, California|url=https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/disney-music-group-set-to-release-first-6-remastered-star-wars-original-motion-picture-soundtracks-on-may-4-300617275.html|text=Disney Music Group Set To Release First 6 Remastered Star Wars Original Motion Picture Soundtracks On May 4|date=2018-03-21|archivedate=20230102013031|agency=PR Newswire}}</ref>

==Track listing for the American program==

===LP===
Side One:
# Star Wars (Main Theme) – 5:49 
##0:00-1:30 : [1m1] Main Title
## 1:30-3:30 : [1m2] The Imperial Probe (microedited, missing 280 seconds)
##3:30-end : [6m3] This is Not a Cave (microedited, missing 80 seconds)
#Yoda's Theme – 3:24
##0:00-end : Yoda's Theme
#The Training of a Jedi Knight – 3:08 
##0:00-0:45 : [6m4] Training a Jedi (microedited, missing 55 seconds)
## 0:45-end : [5m3] Yoda's Entrance (microedited, missing 10 seconds)
#The Heroics of Luke and Han – 6:18
##0:00-2:47 : [1m3-2m1] Luke's Escape (microedited, missing 44 seconds)
##2:47-end : [4m2] The Rebels Escape Again

##0:00-end : Darth Vader's Theme

##0:00-3:17 : [10m3] Chewie Chokes Lando (microedited, missing 44 seconds)
##3:17-end : [9m3] Putting Threepio Together (microedited, missing 50 seconds)
# Han Solo and the Princess – 3:25
##0:00-end : [5m4-6m1] Solo and the Princess
#Hyperspace – 4:02 
##0:00-end : [12m2] To Hyper-space
#The Battle in the Snow – 3:48 
##0:00-end : [3m3] The Snow Battle

Side Three:

#The City in the Clouds – 6:29 
##0:00-2:07 : [6m2] Yoda's Teaching
##2:07-end : [8m2] City in the Clouds (microedited, missing 24 seconds)
#Rebels at Bay – 5:23
## 0:00-1:37 : [2m3] Luke's Rescue
##1:37-end : [3m4-4m1] Luke's First Crash (microedited, missing 27 seconds)
#Yoda and the Force – 4:01
##0:00-end : [7m3] Yoda Raises the Ship

Side Four: 
#The Duel – 4:07
##0:00-end : [11m1] Through the Window (microedited, missing 5 seconds)
#The Magic Tree – 3:32
##0:00-end : [6m5-7m1] The Magic Tree
# Lando's Palace – 3:52
##0:00-end : [8m3] Lando's Palace
#Finale – 6:28
##0:00-1:57 : [12m3] Finale
##1:57-end : [12m4] End Credits

'''Total Time''': 74:34
